svg.title: Cassette grounding and bonding architecture
svg.desc: Single-point ground at ELEC end with platform SPG connected via 50 mm² bond cable to ECP ground stud to Cassette SPG bar. All metallic components bond to SPG: 15 racks via 25 mm² braid, CDU via 35 mm² cable, load-spreader plate welded to container frame, cable trays, access panels with EMI gaskets, and both ECP covers. The 800 V DC bus is not bonded (ungrounded IT per IEC 61557-8). Offshore variant adds seam jumpers on every bolted seam for RFI suppression.
svg.text: Cassette grounding and bonding — single-point ground architecture
All bonds converge at one SPG bar. 800 V DC bus is NOT bonded (ungrounded IT per IEC 61557-8).
What is NOT bonded to SPG — critical

g.text: 800 V DC + and − conductors are NOT connected to SPG bar or container frame
This is the defining feature of an ungrounded IT system (IEC 61557-8)
Bender IMD measures insulation resistance from each pole to ground
First ground fault → no fault current, IMD alarms, system keeps running
Bonding the DC bus to SPG would DESTROY this safety property
Not just the single ECP ground stud
Insulation resistance ≥ 1 GΩ at 1 kV megger (before main disconnect close)
Confirms ungrounded DC bus isolation is intact before energization
Bender IMD continuous monitoring takes over post-energize

svg.text: Single-point ground discipline: all bonds converge at one bar. No parallel paths to ground elsewhere.
Parallel paths create ground loops → ground potential differences → high-speed I/O corruption, DC offset.
This is the reason ELEC ECP is the only ground penetration, and why ELEC ECP and CDU ECP are on opposite ends.
